q: What is the process of sending voice, speech, music, or images intended for reception by the general public?

In NTSC television standards, how many horizontal scanning lines are there per frame?
In PAL and SECAM television standards, how many horizontal scanning lines are there per frame?
Why is interlaced scanning used in traditional analog television systems?
a: To increase the horizontal resolution of the picture
b: To eliminate flicker without increasing the video signal bandwidth
c: To simplify the design of the receiver's local oscillator
d: To separate the color information from the black-and-white data

What is the vertical frame-scanning frequency in standard monochrome NTSC television?
What is the horizontal scanning line frequency in standard monochrome NTSC television?
What is the horizontal scanning line frequency in standard color NTSC television?
What is the exact vertical frame-scanning frequency in standard color NTSC television?
What name is given to the black-and-white or brightness component of a composite video signal?
In television, what is the letter designation used to represent the luminance signal?
What mathematical matrix represents the composition of the luminance (Y) signal from the primary colors (Red, Green, Blue) in NTSC?
What are the two color-difference quadrature signals used to represent chrominance in the NTSC color TV system?
What is the exact frequency of the color subcarrier used for chrominance transmission in the NTSC system?
What is the exact frequency of the color subcarrier used in the PAL television system?
What is the purpose of the color burst signal in composite video waveforms?
a: To control the brightness of the screen
b: To synchronize the phase and frequency of the receiver's local 3.58 MHz subcarrier oscillator
c: To trigger the horizontal deflection sweep
d: To blank the picture tube during retrace

q: Where is the color burst signal located in the composite video waveform?
a: On the front porch of the horizontal blanking pulse
b: On the back porch of the horizontal blanking pulse
c: During the active trace portion of the line
d: On the peak of the horizontal sync pulse

q: How many cycles of the color subcarrier are typically transmitted during the color burst interval?
What circuit in a color television receiver is used to separate the horizontal sync pulses from the composite video signal?
What passive circuit filter is used to extract the vertical sync pulses from the sync separator output?
What passive circuit filter is used to extract the horizontal sync pulses from the sync separator output?
What is the function of the equalizing pulses transmitted during the vertical blanking interval?
a: To equalize the brightness of successive fields
b: To maintain correct interlacing by equalizing the charge in the integrator before vertical retrace
c: To amplify the color subcarrier signal
d: To trigger the audio de-emphasis circuit

q: What is the function of the serrations in the vertical sync pulse?
a: To prevent the picture from blooming
b: To keep the horizontal sweep oscillator in synchronization during the vertical sync period
c: To blank the screen at the bottom of the raster

q: What is the term for the time period during which the electron beam in a picture tube is turned off as it flies back from right to left or bottom to top?

Which camera tube utilizes lead oxide (PbO) as its photoconductive target material, providing minimal lag and high resolution?

In a standard 6-MHz NTSC channel, how far below the upper channel edge is the aural (sound) carrier frequency located?

What is the difference between the sound carrier and the color subcarrier frequencies in an NTSC channel?
In television receiver designs, what is the intercarrier sound method?
a: Demodulating the audio directly at the RF tuner stage
b: Using the 4.5-MHz beat frequency produced by mixing the visual and aural carriers to detect the sound information
c: Transmitting the audio on an AM subcarrier
d: Eliminating the sound carrier completely using DSP

q: In standard NTSC television, the color difference signals I and Q are modulated in quadrature onto the color subcarrier using what modulation technique?

a: It increases the high voltage of the anode
b: It demagnetizes the shadow mask to restore correct color purity
c: It filters out high frequency power line hum

What represents the 'front porch' of the composite video horizontal blanking interval?
a: The area of the signal before the horizontal blanking pulse starts
b: The brief interval of blanking level immediately before the start of the horizontal sync pulse
c: The interval containing the color burst

q: What represents the 'back porch' of the horizontal blanking interval?
a: The interval containing the horizontal sync pulse
b: The blanking level period after the horizontal sync pulse but before the start of active video
c: The start of the vertical blanking pulse
d: The blanking level immediately preceding the sync pulse
